screen.title: Recipes
screen.subtitle: Matched to your diet — save the ones you love and tick them off as you cook.
screen.empty: Choose a dietary preference during onboarding to get matched recipes — or save any you like here.

veg-stirfry.title: Rainbow veggie stir-fry
veg-stirfry.desc: Crisp seasonal vegetables tossed in a quick savory sauce. Ready in 20 minutes.
veg-stirfry.nutrition: Loaded with fiber and color; naturally plant-based.
ingredients: 1 tbsp tamari or gluten-free soy sauce
steps: Heat the oil in a wide pan over high heat.
Add garlic, then the vegetables; stir-fry 5–7 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add the tamari, toss to coat, and serve over rice.

chickpea-curry.title: Spiced chickpea curry
chickpea-curry.desc: Hearty, warming and protein-rich — great for batch cooking.
chickpea-curry.nutrition: High in plant protein and fiber; steadies blood sugar.
steps: Soften the onion in a little oil, then stir in the curry powder.
Add tomatoes, coconut milk and chickpeas; simmer 15 minutes.
Season to taste and serve with rice or flatbread.

lentil-soup.title: Cozy red lentil soup
lentil-soup.desc: A simple one-pot soup that's filling and budget-friendly.
lentil-soup.nutrition: Plant protein plus fiber; gentle and gluten-free.
steps: Sweat the onion and carrot for a few minutes.
Add lentils, stock and cumin; bring to a boil.
Simmer 20 minutes until the lentils break down, then blend if you like.

quinoa-salad.title: Lemony quinoa salad
quinoa-salad.desc: Bright, no-cook-after-chilling salad that travels well.
quinoa-salad.nutrition: Complete plant protein; fresh and gluten-free.
steps: Combine the quinoa, cucumber and tomatoes in a bowl.
Whisk the lemon juice and olive oil, then toss through.
Season and chill for 10 minutes before serving.

overnight-oats.title: Overnight oats
overnight-oats.desc: Stir at night, grab in the morning. Five-minute prep.
overnight-oats.nutrition: Slow-release carbs and fiber; no added sugar.
steps: Mix everything in a jar.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Top with fruit in the morning and enjoy cold.

greek-yogurt-bowl.title: Protein yogurt bowl
greek-yogurt-bowl.desc: A five-minute high-protein breakfast or snack.
greek-yogurt-bowl.nutrition: High protein, low sugar; keeps you full.
steps: Spoon the yogurt into a bowl.
Top with berries and nuts.
Drizzle with honey if you like and serve.

salmon-bowl.title: Salmon & greens bowl
salmon-bowl.desc: Omega-rich salmon over greens and grains.
steps: Pan-sear or bake the salmon until just cooked through.
Build the bowl with greens and grains.
Top with salmon and avocado, finish with lemon.

tuna-salad.title: Quick tuna salad
tuna-salad.nutrition: Lean protein, low sugar; very quick.
steps: Flake the tuna over the salad leaves.
Add the beans.
Dress with olive oil and lemon, then toss.

chicken-veg.title: Sheet-pan chicken & veg
chicken-veg.desc: Everything roasts on one tray — minimal cleanup.
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C / 400°F.
Toss the chicken and vegetables with oil and herbs on a tray.
Roast 25–30 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.

turkey-wrap.title: Turkey & hummus wrap
turkey-wrap.nutrition: Lean protein with fiber to stay full.
steps: Spread the hummus over the wrap.
Layer the turkey, spinach and tomato.
Roll up tightly, slice in half and serve.
